reasoning: Without search capability, I am relying on historical data and geometric Brownian motion estimates. At current prices (around $60k-$70k depending on the specific early 2024 benchmark date), reaching $140,000 by the end of 2025 requires roughly a 2x increase. Using a lognormal random walk model with a high annualized volatility (~70%) and assuming a moderate post-halving drift, the probability of hitting a high of $140k within this timeframe is approximately 25-35%. While post-halving bull runs have historically produced large multiples, diminishing returns suggest that reaching $140k (which is twice the previous cycle's ATH) is a plausible but not guaranteed cycle peak. I estimate the probability at 30%.
